_note: LLMs comparison data. 2026-Q2 snapshot. Pricing changes — lastCheckedAt fields are the source of truth.

pricing.note: Input cache discount lowers effective cost on repeat calls.

mcpSupport.note: Claude was the canonical MCP client; tool-calling is native.

axes.verdict: Default for non-trivial agentic work that lives outside ChatGPT.

items.name: GPT-5
items.primaryJob: Default reasoning + tool-calling on the OpenAI side

pricing.note: Reasoning effort settings can multiply token use 2-3x.

mcpSupport.note: OpenAI's Apps SDK builds on MCP-style servers; native tool calling also works directly.

lagsAt: Fewer cross-vendor host integrations than Claude
axes.verdict: Default if your audience lives in ChatGPT, or if you need image/audio out of the box.

contextWindow.note: Context windows above ~200k degrade in real-world recall — measure for your use case.

mcpSupport.note: Vertex AI Agents adopted MCP; Gemini API itself supports function calling natively.

lagsAt: Tool-calling reliability still trails Claude / GPT-5 on agentic loops
axes.verdict: Use when your task is genuinely long-context or genuinely multimodal. Otherwise the field is closer than vendor decks suggest.

pricing.note: Real cost is GPU hours. ~$0.20-0.60/M tokens depending on infra. Tools like Ollama / LM Studio make local viable; production needs bigger boxes.

mcpSupport.note: MCP support depends on your host — Cline / Continue / Goose handle MCP for any local model.

lagsAt: Reasoning on hardest tasks trails frontier closed models
axes.verdict: Default when data residency or vendor lock-in is non-negotiable. For anything else, the closed-model gap is meaningful.
